questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.28]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (15,0);\coordinate (C) at (0,8);\draw (A)--(B) node[midway,below,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$15$};\draw (A)--(C) node[midway,left,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$8$};\draw (B)--(C) node[midway,above right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$x$};\draw (1,0)--(1,1)--(0,1);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
The two shorter sides of a right-angled triangle are $8$ and $15$. Which calculation finds the hypotenuse $x$?

options.why: Subtracted the squares, treating a shorter side as if it were the hypotenuse.
Added the squares but forgot to take the square root.
Added the two sides without squaring them first.

questions.solution_text:
The hypotenuse is found by adding the squares of the two shorter sides.
$x=\sqrt{8^{2}+15^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{289}$
$=17$

questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.45]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (3,0);\coordinate (C) at (0,9.54);\draw (A)--(B) node[midway,below,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$3\text{ m}$};\draw (A)--(C) node[midway,left,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$x$};\draw (B)--(C) node[midway,above right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$10\text{ m}$};\draw (0.5,0)--(0.5,0.5)--(0,0.5);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
A $10\text{ m}$ ladder rests against a wall with its foot $3\text{ m}$ from the wall. How high up the wall does it reach? Round to $2$ decimal places.

options.why: Added the squares instead of subtracting, finding a hypotenuse rather than a shorter side.
Subtracted the lengths ($10-3$) without squaring them.
Correct value but written in centimetres instead of metres.

questions.solution_text:
The ladder is the hypotenuse, so the height is a shorter side.
$x=\sqrt{10^{2}-3^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{91}$
$\approx9.54\text{ m}$

questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=1.0]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (1.5,0);\coordinate (C) at (1.5,2);\coordinate (D) at (0,2);\draw (A)--(B)--(C)--(D)--cycle;\draw (A)--(C) node[midway,above left,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$x$};\draw (A)--(B) node[midway,below,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$1.5\text{ m}$};\draw (B)--(C) node[midway,right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$2.0\text{ m}$};\draw (1.3,0)--(1.3,0.2)--(1.5,0.2);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
A rectangular gate is $1.5\text{ m}$ wide and $2.0\text{ m}$ high. Find the length of the diagonal brace $x$.

options.why: Subtracted the squares instead of adding, as if finding a shorter side.
Added the two sides without squaring them first.
Added the squares but forgot to take the square root.

questions.solution_text:
The brace is the hypotenuse of the right-angled triangle.
$x=\sqrt{1.5^{2}+2.0^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{6.25}$
$=2.5\text{ m}$

questions.question_text: The numbers $9$, $40$ and $x$ form a Pythagorean triad, where $x$ is the largest number. Find $x$.

options.why: Subtracted the squares instead of adding, treating $40$ as the hypotenuse.
Added the two numbers ($9+40$) without squaring them.
Added the squares but forgot to take the square root.

questions.solution_text:
The largest number is the hypotenuse.
$x=\sqrt{9^{2}+40^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{1681}$
$=41$

questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.7]\coordinate (A) at (0,0);\coordinate (B) at (5,0);\coordinate (C) at (2.5,4.33);\coordinate (M) at (2.5,0);\draw (A)--(B)--(C)--cycle;\draw[dashed] (C)--(M) node[midway,right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$h$};\draw (A)--(C) node[midway,above left,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$8\text{ cm}$};\draw (B)--(C) node[midway,above right,fill=white,inner sep=1pt]{$8\text{ cm}$};\node[below] at (1.25,0) {$4\text{ cm}$};\node[below] at (3.75,0) {$4\text{ cm}$};\draw (2.5,0.28)--(2.78,0.28)--(2.78,0);\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
Find the exact perpendicular height $h$ of an equilateral triangle with side length $8\text{ cm}$.

options.why: Added the squares instead of subtracting when finding the height (a shorter side).
Halved every length first, using $\sqrt{4^{2}-2^{2}}$ instead of $\sqrt{8^{2}-4^{2}}$.
Used the slanted side length as the height without any calculation.

questions.solution_text:
The height splits the triangle into two right-angled triangles, each with base $4\text{ cm}$.
$h=\sqrt{8^{2}-4^{2}}$
$=\sqrt{48}\text{ cm}$
